jobs.title: Planning Specialist
jobs.description:
We are looking for a Planning Specialist to work with a large advisory team in the Cincinnati Market. The ideal candidate is highly motivated, exceptionally organized, and has a passion for helping others create sound financial plans.

This position works closely with the senior Wealth Management Advisor in charge of planning for the team. Responsibilities include:
• Engaging with clients and prospects to help them understand and articulate their financial goals with respect to retirement, education, wealth preservation, and other.
• Developing coherent and realistic financial plans that address the interrelated concerns – risk tolerance, growth requirements, time frames, investment instruments and structure, tax sensitivity, legal/regulatory considerations, etc. – involved in complex planning situations.
• Quantifying and modeling financial goals to clarify and illuminate decision points for advisor use and presentation to clients.
• Collect and input data into a range of planning software. Create reports, spreadsheets, etc. based on that data for presentation to clients.
• Proactive collaboration with team members to maintain a positive, productive team environment.

If you enjoy financial planning, and want to work with a respected, successful and engaged team committed to providing valuable, top-tier, planning advice to others, this position may be the one for you.

jobs.title: Global Markets – Agency Algorithmic Trading – Quantitative Analyst

titleByLocale.it: Global Markets – Agency Algorithmic Trading – Quantitative Analista
titleByLocale.en: Global Markets – Agency Algorithmic Trading – Quantitative Analyst
titleByLocale.de: Global Markets – Agency Algorithmic Trading – Quantitative Analyst
titleByLocale.fr: Global Markets – Agency Algorithmic Trading – Quantitative Analyste

jobs.description:
We’re looking for a quantitative analyst to support algorithmic trading platform and client analytics and quantitative configuration across APAC markets. The role combines platform level quantitative work with production analytics, transaction cost analysis (TCA), and client facing performance review support. You will:
• Develop and maintain quantitative frameworks that influence algorithmic trading behaviour, including market specific configuration and analytics logic.
• Enhance analytics pipelines used for algorithm monitoring and performance analysis, including helping drive data availability and re processing requirements with relevant data teams.
• Perform execution performance analysis and transaction cost analysis (TCA) for internal and client facing reviews.
• Analyse execution behaviour across market conditions, arrival times, and execution styles, translating results into clear, actionable insights, client specific and broader platform / algo improvements and internalization.
• Support methodology discussions and follow up analysis related to analytics outputs and benchmarks as well as providing market microstructure based insights.
• Partner with Trading, Product, Technology, and Control teams to ensure analytics outputs align with platform behaviour and business requirements.

jobs.title: Market risk model validation Quantitative Analyst

titleByLocale.it: Market risk model validation Quantitative Analista
titleByLocale.en: Market risk model validation Quantitative Analyst
titleByLocale.de: Market risk model validation Quantitative Analyst
titleByLocale.fr: Market risk model validation Quantitative Analyste

jobs.description:
We are looking for a Market risk quant in the Market risk model validation team in India to perform:
• Independent validation of market risk models in line with the UBS model governance policy and regulatory requirements. Key responsibilities include
• Assess the model's conceptual soundness and methodology
• Check the appropriateness/quality of input data used for model development, review appropriateness of model parameters, accuracy of the model calibration etc.
• Review model outcome, impacts, or develop alternate benchmark approaches to challenge model outputs (as appropriate)
• Assess model risk, providing effective challenge to model assumptions and evaluate model limitations
• Write comprehensive validation reports, documenting the assessments to required standards
• Collaborate effectively with stakeholders globally to understand / challenge potential model risks; communicate validation findings to relevant stakeholders
• Support ongoing review of model performance and model change reviews to ensure models remain fit for purpose

jobs.title: Sachbearbeiter /in Kindes- und Erwachsenenschutzrecht
jobs.description:
Haben Sie Interesse an Themen mit rechtlichem Bezug und eine gute Auffassungsgabe? Sind effizientes und exaktes Arbeiten Ihre Stärken? Suchen Sie den Einstieg in die Finanzbranche?
Wir suchen eine/n Sachbearbeiter/in Kindes- und Erwachsenenschutzrecht als Verstärkung für unser Team mit folgenden Aufgaben:
• Analyse und Bearbeitung von behördlichen Massnahmen der Kindes- und Erwachsenenschutzbehörde mittels Recherche und Mutationen im eigenen Banksystem
• Abwicklung von nicht-behördlichen Massnahmen in Form von Vorsorgeaufträgen und Vertretung von Ehegatten
• Durchführung von Kindsvermögenssicherungsmassnahmen
• Formelle sowie materielle Kontrolle der Legitimationen wie auch Dokumentation der Massnahmen
• Telefonische und schriftliche Unterstützung von Kundenberater/Innen bezüglich fachlicher Anfragen
• Telefonischer und schriftlicher Kontakt mit Behörden, Ämtern, Anwälten wie auch Notaren
• Allgemeine administrative Arbeiten

jobs.description:
Are you able to handle multiple priorities (combination of new and repetitive) with energy and enthusiasm? Are you detail oriented, even when multitasking? We’re looking for someone like that to help us with:
*acknowledge, verify and validate client and fund contribution, withdrawal, redemption and subscription notifications
* work with Portfolio Managers on contribution/withdrawals requests
*ensure timely and accurate booking of cash flow activity
*actively investigate and reconcile breaks against custody records

jobs.title: Global banking - Investment Banking Associate
jobs.description:
Are you a leader in innovation? Do you know how to come up with creative ideas for clients? We’re looking for an Associate in the Global Markets to :

•take lead in the execution of mandates working with cross functional teams and senior bankers. Carry out valuations, financial modeling, credit, capital structure and financial returns analysis & lead on transaction execution
• •work with senior coverage – sector and product bankers to come up with new ideas to pitch to companies.
• •work with industry / corporate finance team to execute transactions, and position companies, liaison with syndicate / sales team to market transaction.
• •prepare and present materials such as information memorandums and presentations for boards of directors, internal review committees and solicitations (pitches)
• •assessment and evaluation of key trends and opportunities for the specific sectors.
• •help to drive the client franchise by working with our sector groups and global offices to generate opportunities of relevance and clients.
•you will be encouraged to mentor, guide and develop Analysts/ ENOs, help coordinate Analyst/ENOs work on projects, develop them through delegation of responsibility, and takes a prominent role in training initiatives.
•deliver continuous process improvements, in view of the changing operating and regulatory environment

jobs.title: Data Engineer-Python, Pyspark, sql, Databricks
jobs.description:
Are you passionate about data engineering? Are you keen to manage data that can materially help teams improve the way they work? You have the opportunity to join the team working on the new DevLens initiative, an ambitious data initiative to help our 40,000 IT employees organization accelerate value delivery to customers, improve organization efficiency and developer experience. Together with your team you will contribute to building and maintaining innovative data products spanning all engineering activities across UBS IT lines of business.

You will work with large delta tables on Azure Databricks with Dagster, AKS, PySpark & SparkSQL, Confluent Kafka, REST APIs, database platforms, serving data products on Starburst/Trino data mesh.

We’re looking for a passionate Data Engineer to:
• Develop, support and improve data pipelines and data products with attention to governance including sourcing, lineage, modelling, security, quality, distribution and efficiency
• Automate testing & deployment where possible using CI/CD pipelines
• Build observability to monitor and resolve production issues
• Analyse and organize raw data and be able to combine multiple datasets of varying quality
• Take ownership and drive deliveries within a supportive team environment
• Follow engineering best practices, and ensure bank & regulatory compliance across the lifecycle
• Ensure the quality, security, reliability, and compliance of our solutions, promote re-use where possible.
• Help manage the department data inventory and data products
• Provide data support to internal & external stakeholders
• Be comfortable within a geographically spread, fast-moving Agile team
• Continuously up-skill, learn new technologies and practices, reuse strategic platforms and standards, evaluate options, and make decisions with long-term sustainability in mind.

You will work with large delta tables on Azure Databricks with Dagster, AKS, PySpark & SparkSQL, Confluent Kafka, REST APIs, database platforms, serving data products on Starburst/Trino data mesh. We’re looking for a passionate Data Engineer to:
• Develop, support and improve data pipelines and data products with attention to governance including sourcing, lineage, modelling, security, quality, distribution and efficiency
• Automate testing & deployment where possible using CI/CD pipelines
• Build observability to monitor and resolve production issues
• Analyse and organize raw data and be able to combine multiple datasets of varying quality
• Take ownership and drive deliveries within a supportive team environment
• Follow engineering best practices, and ensure bank & regulatory compliance across the lifecycle
• Ensure the quality, security, reliability, and compliance of our solutions, promote re-use where possible.
• Help manage the department data inventory and data products
• Provide data support to internal & external stakeholders
• Be comfortable within a geographically spread, fast-moving Agile team
• Continuously up-skill, learn new technologies and practices, reuse strategic platforms and standards, evaluate options, and make decisions with long-term sustainability in mind.

jobs.title: Senior Electronic Algorithmic Trading Quantitative Analyst

titleByLocale.it: Senior Electronic Algorithmic Trading Quantitative Analista
titleByLocale.en: Senior Electronic Algorithmic Trading Quantitative Analyst
titleByLocale.de: Senior Electronic Algorithmic Trading Quantitative Analyst
titleByLocale.fr: Senior Electronic Algorithmic Trading Quantitative Analyste

jobs.description:
Are you passionate about performing research, analyzing data and coding up improvements to complex algorithmic trading strategies? We're looking for someone to analyze, adapt and improve the quantitative performance of UBS’s suite of agency algorithmic trading and smart order routing strategies.

The successful candidate will primarily:
• perform thorough research on areas such as market microstructure and market impact analysis.
• perform Transaction Costs Analysis (TCA) to minimize executions costs.
• improve consistency of algo performance and reduce outliers and risk, particularly during significant unscheduled as well as scheduled events e.g. index rebalance events.
• design and spec new trading strategies with a particular focus on low latency trading strategies.
• analyze trading patterns and trends within market data (L2 or L3 format).
• employ modern data analytical tools such as machine learning but in a controlled and practical way.
• adapt algorithms to adjust to new markets, venues, order types, asset classes and regulation.
• generate intraday trading signals and volume forecasts from microseconds to minutes.
• communicate with internal stakeholders and external clients and vendors to understand business requirements and market trends.
• produce analytics and reports to monitor and benchmark trading performance both internally and for clients

jobs.title: SAP Finance Functional Business Lead
jobs.description:
The SAP Business Integration Lead (ED) is accountable for end to end business integration across SAP S/4HANA, FPSL, Group Reporting and SAC within the multi-year ampliFi program. You will be the primary architect of the bridge between banking finance operations and SAP technical execution. The role ensures that business requirements, finance processes, data, and SAP solutions are cohesive, scalable, and aligned to global standards, acting as a senior partner to Design Authority and program leadership. Accountable for ensuring functional integrity, data lineage, controls, and operating model alignment across the finance value chain for a complex multi-national Tier 1 bank.

Key Responsibilities
- Own end to end solution design and integration across Finance domains, ensuring consistency across processes, data flows, and reporting outcomes.
- Act as a senior Design Authority partner, providing direction, challenge, and oversight on solution and integration decisions.
- Translate complex finance and business requirements into integrated SAP S/4HANA designs.
- Oversee integration between SAP and non SAP systems, ensuring clarity on accounting impacts and downstream reporting.
- Partner with Functional, Data, Testing, Deployment, and Change teams to enable successful delivery.
- Engage with senior stakeholders, providing clear insights on risks, dependencies, and design trade offs.
- Translate complex SAP capabilities into business value and vice versa.
- Shape and evolve the future state business integration architecture to support global rollout and long term sustainability.

Required Experience & Skills
- 10+ years of Extensive experience in large scale SAP S/4HANA Finance transformations. Global Deployment experience preferred. - Experience in SAP BTP a plus Banking domain experience (wealth management, investment banking, retail)
- Experience with multi-GAAP and multi-currency environments
- Strong finance domain knowledge (Accounting, Reporting, Controlling, Consolidation).
- Proven track record of leading large-scale global finance transformation (Big4 or equivalent background preferred).
- Ability to operate credibly at senior executive level, influencing and challenging stakeholders.
- Strong communication and leadership skills.

• manage P&L, and incorporate P&L considerations in business decisions

jobs.title: GWM Finance Intern
jobs.description:
Are you interest in Finance? Do you have an eye for detail and have a strong analytical skillset?
We are looking for an intern to:

• prepare weekly and monthly standard reporting for head quarter
• develop analytical controls and integrated reconciliation spreadsheets and processes
• track banker performance at client and product level
• support end users on Business Intelligence tools, supporting on Dashboards preparation/correction and on the tool administrative management
• automate manual processes and assist in the technological development of the financial area
• introduce process improvements and implement financial best practices

The GWM Finance Intern is responsible for providing support with analysis and insight of WM results in coordination with the broader Finance team.
This role will give you the opportunity to build a strong knowledge base across multiple finance specialist areas including accounting, product control and tax planning.
You’ll also support on technical routines related to business intelligence tools as Qlik Sense and Alteryx.

• Drive business growth and assist with the management and development of Financial Advisors

• Coach all team members, getting them the right training and making sure development measures are results-oriented and transparent

jobs.title: Instrument reference Data Specialist
jobs.description:
Data Steward create, maintain & provide access to data. They define key data under their management and implement efficient and effective data management process as well as adequate controls to ensure that data is provided in good quality and in line with UBS policies and commercial agreements.
Key Responsibilities
• Design implement and execute processes to create and maintain data
• Apply a broad range of data management skills, from information management, non-financial risk control, data quality issue management, data cleaning, data governance to ensure that data is properly managed, shared and preserved
• Ensure the quality, security, reliability, and policy compliance of data
• Design, build, and test data quality controls and business rules, monitor data quality and manage rules exceptions.
• Remediate data quality issues
• Apply concepts and assume responsibilities as defined in the Data Management Framework
• Document and continuously improve efficiency and robustness of data management process.
• Manage day-to-day stakeholder interaction.

titleByLocale.en: GWM US Field Talent Development and Delivery Program Manager
titleByLocale.de: GWM US Field Talent Development and Delivery Program Manager
titleByLocale.fr: GWM US Field Talent Development and Delivery Program Manager

jobs.description:
Are you passionate about equipping individuals with the skills to lead, inspire, and motivate teams to drive organizational success? Do you have great judgment on how to grow and develop talent? Are you excited to support our teams on their path to greater responsibilities, territories and businesses? We’re seeking an experienced and highly organized Program Manager to manage and enhance our WM Field Talent Training Programs. This role is critical in ensuring the successful delivery of training and development in Wealth Management.

Key responsibilities:

• Design and deliver leadership and recruiting workshops across all U.S. regions, both virtually and in person
• Strengthen leadership talent pipelines through targeted development, assessment, and readiness initiatives
• Evaluate and deliver high impact leadership programs focused on coaching, communication, and team effectiveness
• Support delivery of programs for existing and new Financial Advisors
• Contribute to the design and execution of a practice management program
• Create business updates for senior leadership
• Lead and strengthen the collective work across Field Leader, Financial Advisor and Field Support training and development

jobs.title: Client Services Intern
jobs.description:
Are you interested in starting your career in the financial industry and gaining hands-on experience in a dynamic and international environment?

We are looking for an Intern to join our RTA (Registrar and Transfer Agent) team, where you will support the Investors team in managing shareholder activities and capital movements of offshore investment funds. Your main responsibilities will include:

• supporting the Investors team in handling shareholder requests
• assisting in the processing and monitoring of capital movements of offshore funds, including subscriptions, redemptions, dividend distributions, and capital returns
• supporting the analysis and monitoring of payments to fund service providers
• assisting in tracking and controlling revenues within the offshore fund structure
• interacting with external portfolio managers and fund service providers
• contributing to daily operational routines and controls related to investor servicing

jobs.title: Broker-dealer Internship
jobs.description:
Are you interested in the broker-dealer risk team? Are you curious and committed? We are looking for someone to:
• work in a dynamic front-office environment along with the trading desks
• assist in developing metrics and processes to mitigate risk
• assist in limit calibration for a wide range of clients
• monitor client trading activity and limits
• quickly solve trading limits related issues
• assist the team to perform risk analysis and deliver best solutions to our clients
• help to run and improve our reports, which cover pre-trading risk, liquidity risk, clients capital monitoring, end-of-day stress calculations and other risk related items
• interact with other UBS teams both onshore and offshore to ensure client needs are met.
• work on the resolution of non-operational client issues and complaints in accordance with internal policies and external regulatory requirements.

titleByLocale.en: Middle Office Funds Intern - Global Wealth Management

jobs.description:
Are you interested in being part of a team that manages the entire operational lifecycle of products traded by investment funds administered by UBS?
We are looking for an intern to join our Middle Office Funds team who will:

• support the oversight of onshore investment fund trades, including performing financial reconciliation routines
• pre-match and transmit settlement files to domestic custodians across equity, futures, stock loan, and fixed income markets
• analyze and resolve discrepancies with domestic managers and counterparties
• monitor and evaluate the impact of dissent, subscriptions, IPOs, and follow-on events (Corporate Actions)
• execute cash control processes for funds engaged in equity, futures, and stock loan transactions

jobs.title: Regulatory reporting specialist
jobs.description:
Are you a person keen to learn and grow in banking operations area?

This role in UBSCL will be mainly responsible for Regulatory reporting to prepare Operations related regulatory reports.
take responsibility for senior role to guide people doing right things and escalate any unmoral cases, keep in mind the UBS risk culture
- pre-check on client documents prepared by client advisor against internal procedure and checklist to ensure the application documents comply with DOA, document provided is in completeness
- contribute to continuous improvement of operation processes and services; ensure operations processes comply with regulatory requirements, guidelines and internal policies
- perform the specific regulatory reporting according to regulatory requirements, on a regular or ad-hoc basis
- support business and enquiries from Markets team on various products and develop the knowledge, skills and experience of team members
- support internal and regional projects as part of role responsibility, Finacle project experience is preferred
- No any compliance and regulations breach

jobs.title: Spezialist/in Hypotheken und Grundbuchwesen 80 – 100%
jobs.description:
Êtes-vous passionné par les prêts bancaires ? Vous êtes autonome, fiable et possédez une grande conscience professionnelle ? Vous appréciez travailler dans un environnement dynamique et stimulant où les objectifs vous motivent ? Si vous avez répondu oui à ces questions, alors rejoignez notre équipe Credit Services et faites partie d'une entreprise où votre talent sera reconnu et valorisé.

En tant que spécialiste crédits, vous serez chargé de :

• traiter les demandes de prêt approuvées à l'échelle nationale, en jouant un rôle clé dans la réalisation des projets de nos clients
• préparer les contrats de crédit avec les documents de sûreté appropriés, assurant ainsi la documentation nécessaire à nos prêts
• saisir et gérer les crédits et garanties (p.ex. les cédules hypothécaires, polices et cautions)
• emettre les promesses de paiement et leur exécution
• assurer la correspondance et le dialogue avec les registres fonciers, notaires, banques et compagnies d’assurance, établissant des relations de confiance avec nos partenaires
• maintenir des contacts professionnels avec les conseillers à la clientèle, les Credit Officers et les services juridiques

jobs.title: Data Scientist
jobs.description:
Are you excited to work with a global team with diverse backgrounds and talents? Can you apply your technical skills to help quantify business risks? Do you enjoy the challenge of driving business impact through analytics and are looking for an opportunity to lead innovation?

We’re looking for a Data Scientist to:

• engage in audits to analyze data using traditional machine learning or Large Language Models to uncover trends, patterns, or anomalies for auditors to investigate further.
• work collaboratively with stakeholders to assist in risk assessments and highlight high-risk areas for deep dives.
• clearly communicate complex insights derived from analytics to senior stakeholders in a manner that is both engaging and impactful.
• stay up to date with advancements in AI, ML, and NLP to implement cutting-edge solutions, such as working with the latest Large Language Models (LLMs).
• provide auditors with training and support, as required, to effectively use data-driven tools and solutions.

jobs.title: Trade & Client Service Specialist
jobs.description:
Are you looking for a new challenge within the fast-paced Wealth Management environment? Do you want to be part of a high performing team? Are you curious, proactive, and always striving to bring positive impact to clients?

We’re looking for a Tade and Client Service Specialist to:

• Support Client Advisors in UK & Jersey
• Process client orders on an execution only basis covering equities, ETF's, fixed income, structured products, FX options, SPI's and hedge funds.
• Ensuring that administrative arrangements and client-related processes are completed accurately
• Carry out daily transaction processing and complex troubleshooting in line with WMUK & Jersey trading rules
• Dealing directly with external and internal clients
• Ensure all risk management controls are adhered to in order to identify and mitigate any potential risk and appropriate management information (MI) is produced
• Manage the input and release of payments for the Jersey business.
• Perform call backs on payments that fall within the relevant thresholds.

jobs.title: Digital Technical Architect
jobs.description:
Do you want to build your digital architecture skills whilst creating impactful applications? Are you interested in being part of an externally recognized engineering community with personal development at its core? Would you like to join a team that gives back to the community and engages with a diverse group of people?

We are looking for a Digital Technology Architect to work with us to execute our vision and help us create an even stronger industry leading organization. Your role will include:

• Working closely with cross-functional team members in a pod or crew to improve (digital) products in line with our Digital Principles and architecture goals.
• You will be identifying, designing and developing n-tier architectures on different layers e.g., enterprise, data, solution, and technology architecture.
• You will also be consulting with business, technology product owners and architects, helping them translate our OKRs (Objectives and Key Results) into backlog items and driving architectural improvements into the roadmap.

jobs.title: Premises Manager
jobs.description:
The Premises Manager is accountable for the end‑to‑end leadership, operational excellence, and strategic oversight of the UBS Hyderabad premises portfolio, comprising multiple locations. The role ensures that all facilities services are delivered safely, efficiently, compliantly, and in line with UBS standards, while providing a high‑quality workplace experience for employees and stakeholders.
The position leads the local facilities operating model, managing Assistant Managers and overseeing multiple third‑party service partners across soft services, engineering, staff restaurants, and transportation. The Premises Manager acts as the primary senior on‑site representative for facilities operations and is a key partner to local business leadership and regional GRESC stakeholders.

Key 
• Responsibilities
Portfolio & Operational Leadership

Lead the Hyderabad premises portfolio across multiple UBS locations, ensuring consistent service delivery, governance, and compliance across sites.
Provide overall direction, prioritization, and decision‑making for all facilities operations, balancing service quality, cost efficiency, safety, and employee experience.
Act as the senior escalation point for operational, safety, service, and vendor‑related issues across the portfolio.

Team Leadership & Organization

Lead and develop the local Facilities team, including Premises Assistant Managers, providing clear direction, coaching, and performance oversight.
Establish a strong “one‑team” operating model between UBS and supplier partners, reinforcing accountability, collaboration, and service ownership.
Build succession depth, capability, and operational resilience within the premises management team.

Vendor, Contract & Service Partner Management

Own governance of all third‑party service partners across:

Soft Services
Engineering and Technical Services
Staff Restaurants and Catering Operations
Staff Transportation Services

Ensure contracts, SLAs, KPIs, and compliance obligations are met consistently across all locations.
Drive supplier performance reviews, continuous improvement initiatives, and corrective actions where service standards are not met.

Workplace Experience & Service Excellence

Champion workplace experience across the Hyderabad portfolio, ensuring facilities services support productivity, well‑being, and employee engagement.

Provide oversight of high‑visibility services such as staff restaurants and transportation, ensuring quality, safety, and reliability.

Support workplace initiatives, events, and site programs in partnership with Corporate Services and local leadership.

Health, Safety, Risk & Compliance

Maintain overall accountability for health, safety, and regulatory compliance across all Hyderabad premises.

Ensure compliance with local statutory requirements, building codes, fire safety norms, and internal UBS policies.

Proactively identify operational and safety risks, ensuring robust controls, audits, and mitigation plans are in place.

Financial & Commercial Management

Own facilities operating budgets for the Hyderabad portfolio, ensuring effective cost control, forecasting, and financial discipline.

Review and approve invoices, monitor spend, and identify optimization opportunities without compromising service quality or safety.

Support business cases for investments, upgrades, or service enhancements where required.

Stakeholder Management & Reporting

Act as the primary Facilities interface for Hyderabad business stakeholders, site leadership, and regional GRESC teams.

Provide clear, structured reporting on service performance, risks, incidents, and improvement initiatives.

Collaborate closely with internal functions such as Security, Technology, Finance, Risk, and HR to ensure integrated service delivery.

Lead and develop the local Facilities team, including Premises Assistant Managers, providing clear direction, coaching, and performance oversight.
Establish a strong “one‑team” operating model between UBS and supplier partners, reinforcing accountability, collaboration, and service ownership.
Build succession depth, capability, and operational resilience within the premises management team.

Vendor, Contract & Service Partner Management

Own governance of all third‑party service partners across:

Soft Services
Engineering and Technical Services
Staff Restaurants and Catering Operations
Staff Transportation Services

Ensure contracts, SLAs, KPIs, and compliance obligations are met consistently across all locations.
Drive supplier performance reviews, continuous improvement initiatives, and corrective actions where service standards are not met.

Workplace Experience & Service Excellence

Champion workplace experience across the Hyderabad portfolio, ensuring facilities services support productivity, well‑being, and employee engagement.

Provide oversight of high‑visibility services such as staff restaurants and transportation, ensuring quality, safety, and reliability.

Support workplace initiatives, events, and site programs in partnership with Corporate Services and local leadership.

Health, Safety, Risk & Compliance

Maintain overall accountability for health, safety, and regulatory compliance across all Hyderabad premises.

Ensure compliance with local statutory requirements, building codes, fire safety norms, and internal UBS policies.

Proactively identify operational and safety risks, ensuring robust controls, audits, and mitigation plans are in place.

Financial & Commercial Management

Own facilities operating budgets for the Hyderabad portfolio, ensuring effective cost control, forecasting, and financial discipline.

Review and approve invoices, monitor spend, and identify optimization opportunities without compromising service quality or safety.

Support business cases for investments, upgrades, or service enhancements where required.

Stakeholder Management & Reporting

Act as the primary Facilities interface for Hyderabad business stakeholders, site leadership, and regional GRESC teams.

Provide clear, structured reporting on service performance, risks, incidents, and improvement initiatives.

Collaborate closely with internal functions such as Security, Technology, Finance, Risk, and HR to ensure integrated service delivery.

jobs.title: Infrastructure Engineer, Events
jobs.description:
We are a group of professionals who enjoy identifying areas of improvement and engineering better solutions. As a crew, we do our best to create a supportive environment where each of us feel appreciated and have a chance to develop professionally.

We are looking for candidates to take the role of Infrastructure Engineer and help us to:

• Engineer, test and deploy features and releases for the IBM (International Business Machines) MQ (Message Queue) product.
• Assist in keeping the IBM MQ Messaging platform stable, performant, secure and compliant with UBS policies.
• Design, develop and deploy the next generation cloud-based ecosystem for the IBM MQ product.

The key responsibilities are:

• Develop and improve application hosting infrastructure services, with a focus on the IBM MQ product and its associated supporting infrastructure.
• Provide hosting and deployment services that are easy to consume and efficient to operate, both on-prem and in the cloud – making effective use of cloud capabilities on the Azure platform.
• Apply a broad range of engineering practices, in an agile context based on Scrum. From analyzing requirements and developing new features to automated testing, deployment, and operations. With emphasis on Gitlab, the key tooling.
• Ensure the quality, security, reliability, and compliance of solutions by applying our digital principles and implementing both functional and non-functional requirements.
• Build observability into our solutions to monitor production health, help to resolve incidents, and remediate the root cause of risks and issues.
• Learn new technologies and practices, reuse strategic platforms and standards, evaluate options, and make decisions with long-term sustainability in mind.

jobs.title: SAP PaPM Lead
jobs.description:
"The PaPM Delivery Lead supports the delivery of the Profitability and Performance Management (PaPM) capability within the ampliFi program, with accountability for ensuring that profitability, performance, and cost outcomes are delivered in line with UBS finance principles, governance, and control standards. The role is responsible for driving disciplined execution across functional design, business model validation, data readiness, testing, and production transition for PaPM scope, ensuring that performance results are transparent, explainable, and fit for management decision‑making.
The role partners closely with Finance, Data, Integration, and Technology stakeholders and acts as a functional representative for Finance and performance consumers within the delivery. It ensures that business requirements for profitability and performance measurement are translated into well‑controlled, sustainable PaPM solutions aligned with the target finance and analytics architecture.

Key Responsibilities
- Support end‑to‑end functional delivery for assigned PaPM workstreams or modules within the ampliFi finance and analytics stack, with clear ownership of outcomes.
- Contribute to and review functional designs and PaPM models to ensure alignment with agreed finance use cases, performance measurement principles, and cost and profitability logic.
- Ensure that PaPM calculations, allocations, and outputs are transparent, explainable, and aligned with Finance expectations and management reporting needs.
- Develop and maintain delivery plans covering functional scope, milestones, dependencies, and impacts across PaPM data sourcing, calculations, and downstream consumption.
- Proactively manage risks, issues, and dependencies (RAID), with a focus on calculation integrity, data quality, business interpretability, and operational readiness, escalating where required.
- Coordinate day‑to‑day delivery across Finance, Data, Integration, Testing, and Deployment teams to ensure coherent end‑to‑end execution and alignment on functional priorities.
- Ensure adherence to agreed delivery standards, finance principles, model design decisions, and change control processes across PaPM scope.
- Support SIT and UAT execution from a functional perspective, including validation of business scenarios, review of calculation results, assessment of defects based on finance impact, and support for business sign‑off.
- Assist with production readiness and cutover preparation, focusing on stability of calculations, consistency of results, reconciliation where applicable, and smooth transition to business‑as‑usual operations.
"

jobs.title: Operations Specialist - Wealth Management Americas
jobs.description:
Are you highly organized, detail oriented, and committed to delivering high quality outcomes? Do you take pride in accuracy, timeliness, and strong controls?
We are looking for highly motivated individuals who will provide reliable operational execution and support across WMA processes. In this role, you contribute directly to service excellence, risk reduction, and client trust by ensuring tasks are completed accurately, controls are followed, and issues are resolved or escalated appropriately.
This role operates within clearly defined procedures and contributes to continuous improvement through observation, feedback, and disciplined execution.
Key Responsibilities
Operational Execution
• Perform day to day operational activities accurately and within defined timelines
• Execute tasks in accordance with documented procedures, policies, and control standards
• Ensure data integrity across systems, records, and reconciliations
• Meet service level expectations and support consistent delivery
Controls & Risk Awareness
• Follow all applicable regulatory, compliance, and internal control requirements
• Identify breaks, exceptions, or errors and ensure timely resolution or escalation
• Maintain required evidence to support audits and reviews
• Contribute to a strong risk and control culture through disciplined execution
Issue Resolution & Escalation
• Investigate routine issues using standard procedures and guidance
• Escalate non standard, high risk, or unresolved matters to senior team members
• Communicate clearly and promptly with internal partners when support is required
Service & Collaboration
• Provide responsive, professional support to internal stakeholders and partner teams
• Build effective working relationships within and across teams
• Participate constructively in team meetings, knowledge sharing, and cross training
Process Improvement & Learning
• Identify opportunities for process efficiency, error reduction, and quality improvement
• Suggest enhancements within own scope based on observations and experience
• Continuously update skills and knowledge relevant to assigned processes and products

In this role, you contribute directly to service excellence, risk reduction, and client trust by ensuring tasks are completed accurately, controls are followed, and issues are resolved or escalated appropriately.
This role operates within clearly defined procedures and contributes to continuous improvement through observation, feedback, and disciplined execution.
Key Responsibilities
Operational Execution
• Perform day to day operational activities accurately and within defined timelines
• Execute tasks in accordance with documented procedures, policies, and control standards
• Ensure data integrity across systems, records, and reconciliations
• Meet service level expectations and support consistent delivery
Controls & Risk Awareness
• Follow all applicable regulatory, compliance, and internal control requirements
• Identify breaks, exceptions, or errors and ensure timely resolution or escalation
• Maintain required evidence to support audits and reviews
• Contribute to a strong risk and control culture through disciplined execution
Issue Resolution & Escalation
• Investigate routine issues using standard procedures and guidance
• Escalate non standard, high risk, or unresolved matters to senior team members
• Communicate clearly and promptly with internal partners when support is required
Service & Collaboration
• Provide responsive, professional support to internal stakeholders and partner teams
• Build effective working relationships within and across teams
• Participate constructively in team meetings, knowledge sharing, and cross training
Process Improvement & Learning
• Identify opportunities for process efficiency, error reduction, and quality improvement
• Suggest enhancements within own scope based on observations and experience
• Continuously update skills and knowledge relevant to assigned processes and products

jobs.title: SAP FPSL& FSDM Lead
jobs.description:
"The FPSL & FSDM Delivery Lead supports the delivery of the Financial Products Subledger (FPSL) and Financial Services Data Management (FSDM) capabilities within the ampliFi program, with accountability for ensuring that finance, accounting, and data outcomes are delivered in line with UBS accounting principles, data standards, and delivery governance. The role is responsible for driving disciplined execution across functional design, business data readiness, testing, and production transition for FPSL and FSDM scope, ensuring consistent and high‑quality outcomes across the ledger and finance data landscape.
The role partners closely with Finance, Data, Integration, and Technology stakeholders and acts as a functional representative for Finance and data consumers within the delivery. It ensures that business requirements are translated into solutions that support accurate accounting, trusted finance data, and sustainable downstream reporting, while maintaining alignment with the target ledger and data architecture.

Key Responsibilities
- Support end‑to‑end functional delivery for assigned FPSL and FSDM workstreams or modules within the ampliFi ledger and finance data stack, with clear ownership of outcomes.
- Contribute to and review functional designs for FPSL and FSDM to ensure correct accounting treatment, posting logic, data definitions, and alignment with agreed ledger principles and finance data models.
- Ensure that FPSL and FSDM solutions support consistent, reconciled, and reliable finance data for downstream reporting, controls, and analytics.
- Develop and maintain delivery plans covering functional scope, milestones, dependencies, and impacts across FPSL–FSDM integration points and downstream consumers.
- Proactively manage risks, issues, and dependencies (RAID), with a focus on financial accuracy, data quality, regulatory expectations, and operational readiness, escalating where required.
- Coordinate day‑to‑day delivery across Finance, Data, Integration, Testing, and Deployment teams to ensure coherent end‑to‑end execution and alignment on functional priorities.
- Ensure adherence to agreed delivery standards, accounting principles, data standards, design decisions, and change control processes across FPSL and FSDM scope.
- Support SIT and UAT execution from a functional perspective, including validation of business scenarios, assessment of data readiness and defects based on finance impact, and support for business sign‑off.
- Assist with production readiness and cutover preparation, focusing on financial continuity, accuracy and reconciliation of balances, stability of finance data, and smooth transition to business‑as‑usual operations."

jobs.title: SAP FPSL Lead
jobs.description:
"The FPSL Delivery Lead supports the delivery of the Financial Products Subledger (FPSL) capability within the ampliFi program, with accountability for ensuring that finance, accounting, and ledger outcomes are delivered in line with UBS accounting principles, control standards, and delivery governance. The role is responsible for driving disciplined execution across functional design, business validation, testing readiness, and production transition for FPSL scope, while balancing delivery pace with quality, risk management, and stakeholder alignment. The role partners closely with Finance, Data, Integration, and Technology stakeholders to ensure high‑quality, on‑time outcomes and acts as a trusted functional representative for Finance within the FPSL delivery.

Key Responsibilities
- Support end‑to‑end functional delivery for assigned FPSL workstreams or modules within the ampliFi ledger landscape, with clear ownership of outcomes.
- Contribute to and review functional designs and configurations to ensure correct accounting treatment, posting logic, balances, and alignment with UBS ledger principles.
- Develop and maintain delivery plans covering functional scope, milestones, dependencies, and impacts to Finance, ensuring transparency and realistic commitments.
- Proactively manage risks, issues, and dependencies (RAID), with particular focus on financial accuracy, regulatory expectations, and operational readiness, escalating where required.
- Coordinate day‑to‑day delivery activities across Finance, Data, Integration, Testing, and Deployment teams, promoting collaboration and accountability across organisational boundaries.
- Ensure adherence to agreed delivery standards, accounting principles, design decisions, and change control processes.
- Support SIT and UAT execution from a functional perspective, including validation of business scenarios, assessment of defects based on finance impact, and support for business sign‑off.
- Assist with production readiness and cutover preparation, focusing on financial continuity, accuracy of balances, and stability of controls, and supporting smooth transition to business‑as‑usual operations.
"

jobs.title: Alternative Investment Senior Specialist (US Shift)
jobs.description:
Are you incredibly organized with an eye for detail? Do you know how to take care of business?

We are looking for someone like that who can

Perform daily functions such as

Provide premium quality service to Financial Advisors and clients invested in Alternative Investment products; ensuring deadlines are always met

Your role will include the preparation and review of Net Asset Value files, as well as ensuring all client account values are updated accurately and timely, by agreed deadlines.

This role offers the chance to grow and learn within a dynamic and fast-paced environment.

Establish yourself as a trusted partner for Financial Advisors and our clients.

Evaluate procedures and processes with focus on continued improvement, reduce risks, strengthen controls and increase field and client satisfaction.

Reconciliation of ledgers, events, and trades

Tracking of key events and capturing transactions

jobs.description:
Do you have an entrepreneurial mindset and are you eager to understand and fulfill our clients’ needs? Do you want to play a key role in transforming our firm into an agile organization?

At UBS, we re-imagine the way we work, the way we connect with each other – our colleagues, clients and partners – and the way we deliver value. Being agile will make us more responsive, more adaptable, and ultimately more innovative.

We’re looking for a Product Manager to:

• co-develop a clear product vision, strategy and roadmap covering the full product development lifecycle (including creation, version and infrastructure upgrades, and eventual decommissioning)
• drive new products and product changes iteratively and continuously together with cross-functional team members in the pod
• act as product owner within an agile team, participating in agile ceremonies and ensuring the product requirements are clear to designers and engineers.
• ensure the product is being tested together with clients to gain insights and ensure readiness to launch before market entry (e.g., UX testing, end user testing and research insights)
• support pod Objective and Key Results and provide relevant product measurements and metrics to meet overall business goals
• ensure the quality and compliance of the product, from also a technical and non-functional requirement (i.e. production stability, performance, maintainability)

• ensure the product is being tested together with clients to gain insights and ensure readiness to launch before market entry (e.g., UX testing, end user testing and research insights)
• support pod Objective and Key Results and provide relevant product measurements and metrics to meet overall business goals
• ensure the quality and compliance of the product, from also a technical and non-functional requirement (i.e. production stability, performance, maintainability)

jobs.title: Scrum Master
jobs.description:
Do you have a knack for technology? Are you at your best when supporting others?
We are seeking a highly experienced Scrum Master & Agile Project Lead to join our technology team in a mission-critical financial environment. As a Scrum Master, you will lead Agile teams (Pods) through the delivery lifecycle, ensuring adherence to Agile principles and practices. This role is ideal for someone who has a proven track record as a servant leader, removing impediments, fostering collaboration, and driving continuous improvement in regulated industries such as banking or financial services. You will also support Agile transformation initiatives across the Stream.
